title: SonarQube tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: SonarQube project/user token detected in source code. SonarQube project and user tokens (sqp_ prefix) provide API access to SonarQube and SonarCloud platforms for project-level operations and personal authentication. These tokens grant significant permissions including: accessing and analyzing code quality metrics and analysis results, viewing and managing issues, bugs, and code smells, accessing security vulnerabilities and security hotspots, configuring project-level quality gates and quality profiles, managing project settings and configurations, creating and modifying project branches and pull requests, accessing code coverage and test results, managing project webhooks and integrations, commenting on and assigning issues, accessing historical analysis data and trends, managing project members and permissions (project-level), running code analysis scans and uploading results, accessing project portfolios and applications, managing project-level tags and metadata, and accessing API endpoints for automated workflows. Exposing SonarQube project/user tokens enables attackers to: access sensitive code analysis data including vulnerabilities and quality metrics, view source code analysis and potential security weaknesses, modify project configurations and quality gates, manipulate issue statuses to hide vulnerabilities, access historical code quality trends and technical debt information, create false analysis results or manipulate metrics, access project team information and member details, inject malicious webhooks for data exfiltration, consume API rate limits disrupting legitimate analysis, access integration credentials for version control systems, tamper with quality gate decisions allowing vulnerable code deployment, access organization-level information if token has elevated permissions, and use the token to pivot to other projects within the same organization. This is a critical security vulnerability requiring immediate remediation. Take immediate action: revoke the exposed token immediately from SonarQube/SonarCloud (User → My Account → Security → Tokens or Project → Administration → Permissions → Tokens), generate a new token with minimal necessary scopes and permissions, update all applications, CI/CD pipelines, and scripts with the new token, review SonarQube audit logs for unauthorized access or suspicious activity, check which projects and resources the exposed token had access to, verify no quality gates or security rules were modified, review all recent issue changes and comments for tampering, audit webhook configurations for unauthorized endpoints, implement principle of least privilege (use project-level tokens instead of user tokens when possible), store SonarQube tokens in secure secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault, GitHub Secrets), use environment variables with restricted access for automation, never commit SonarQube tokens to version control or configuration files, implement secret scanning in git hooks and CI/CD pipelines, use service accounts with appropriate scopes for automation tasks, rotate SonarQube tokens regularly (every 90 days recommended), enable SAML/SSO and enforce MFA for user accounts, monitor API usage for anomalies and unusual patterns, implement IP whitelisting for API access when feasible, document all tokens in a secure credential inventory, use project-specific tokens to limit blast radius, educate development teams about proper token handling and security, integrate SonarQube token detection in pre-commit hooks, and regularly audit token permissions and access patterns for compliance.
